content.heroQuote: You've built the Databricks gold layer — so why are the answers still a manual export?
content.heroSubtext: Grant, your data infrastructure is nearly there. Milo connects directly to your Databricks gold layer so you, your CFO and your HR lead can all ask questions in plain English. No Looker licences, no SQL, no extra Caliber requests.

keyMetrics.label: to answer what used to need a Caliber data request
data sources connected from day one

clientQuestions.question: How many players were newly acquired, retained and reactivated last month — and how does that split across Casino, Lottery and Sports?
Which games drove the highest GGR last weekend, and are the players they attracted betting at normal stake levels or are they bonus hunters?
Since the last bonus campaign, have the new signups bet at normal levels — or are they the low-value bettors that cost more than they return?
Once Xero goes live on July 1st, what does P&L look like alongside game performance — and can the CFO see that without touching HR payroll data?
What analysis can Milo do directly from the Databricks gold layer that currently needs a Caliber request or a manual export?
Is it worth implementing Looker Studio alongside Milo — or does Milo replace the reporting layer entirely for Grant's team?

solutionHighlights.benefit: Milo reads directly from your gold layer. Caliber's work feeds straight into it — no new pipelines, no duplicate storage.
Grant, your CFO and your HR lead all ask in plain English. Role-based permissions mean everyone sees exactly what they should.
GCP, Databricks, Xero, Deputy, BambooHR. Milo connects to the stack you've already committed to.

relevantCaseStudy.result: Milo replaced manual BI reporting entirely. The team went from raising tickets to getting answers on game performance and player cohorts in minutes.

demoResponses.text: 3 segments identified. Reactivated players are up 18% month-on-month — strong signal from the weekend tournament.
Neon Party is your top GGR earner at $12.4K. Bahamas Happy Hour has the most volume but the lowest average stake — worth reviewing.
Post-bonus average stakes dropped 40% in week 1 then recovered by week 3. The campaign attracted lower-value bettors initially — pattern normalised.
3 of 5 sources are ready to connect today. Xero goes live July 1st. Deputy needs one API key from Grant's team.
Milo handles 4 of the 5 most common Caliber requests directly. Schema changes stay with Caliber — everything else moves to Milo.
Milo replaces Looker for Grant's whole team — with role-based access baked in. CFO sees financials, HR sees people data, Grant sees everything.
